hypothesis: If an LLM agent equipped with iterative feedback and domain-specific external tools (memory, periodic table, and knowledge base) is used to guide compositional search for inorganic materials, then it will achieve higher success rates in targeting specific formation energies and produce more valid, unique, and novel compositions than both tool-free LLM variants and purely generative diffusion-based approaches, because the structured retrieval and iterative refinement mechanisms enable the agent to leverage accumulated chemical knowledge and prior evaluation feedback to systematically converge on stable, property-matched compositions.
